title: Theoretical White-Paper Outline
content:
Theoretical White-Paper Outline
The following outline structures a prospective white-paper, articulating principles, models, and metaphors that underpin the RLOS. It balances rigor with imaginative speculation, framing the system as a “symbolic sentence engine” — a dynamic entity where language layers coalesce into emergent meaning.
1. Introduction: Principles and Foundational Metaphors
• Core Principles: Universality in multilingualism and multimodality; modularity for extensibility; duality of RAW (exploratory, unconstrained) and PUBLIC (constrained, ethical) modes; integration of semiotic translation across symbols, sounds, and visuals.
• Models: A layered semiotic lattice model, where each linguistic layer forms a node in a graph, connected by translational edges (e.g., phonological forms map to semantic meanings via syntactic pathways).
• Metaphors: The RLOS as a “typographic ecology,” where linguistic elements “bloom” like organisms in an adaptive ecosystem, evolving through “language drift” and meta-learning. Alternatively, as a “rhetorical prism,” refracting input language into multifaceted outputs (e.g., persuasive visualizations).
